inputs.label: Number of Samples per Batch
inputs.businessContext: Number of samples taken from a batch for analysis. Based on ISO 2859 (sampling procedures for inspection by attributes).

inputs.label: Batch Size
inputs.businessContext: Total number of units in the batch. Used to calculate sampling ratio and defect probability.

inputs.label: Cost per Sample Analysis
inputs.businessContext: Direct cost of analyzing one sample (labor, reagents, equipment depreciation). Based on laboratory accounting standards.

inputs.label: Historical Defect Rate
inputs.businessContext: Proportion of defective units in historical production. Used for risk-based sampling optimization. Six Sigma DMAIC metric.

inputs.label: Cost per Defective Unit (if undetected)
inputs.businessContext: Cost incurred if a defective unit reaches the customer (rework, scrap, warranty, reputation). Based on quality cost accounting (COPQ).

inputs.label: Sampling Mode
inputs.businessContext: Type of sampling plan: fixed (constant sample size), variable (sample size depends on batch size), sequential (adaptive based on results). ISO 2859 and ANSI/ASQ Z1.4.

inputs.label: Data Confidence Level
inputs.businessContext: Confidence level for statistical estimates. Typically 0.90, 0.95, or 0.99. Used to adjust risk calculations.

thresholds.defectRate > 0.1: High defect rate detected; consider process improvement.
thresholds.costPerSample > 100: Sample cost is high; evaluate alternative methods.

formulas.optimalSampleSize: if samplingMode == 'fixed' then numSamples else if samplingMode == 'variable' then ceil(0.1 * batchSize) else ceil((1 - dataConfidence) * batchSize / (1 - defectRate))

suggestedActions: if defectRate > 0.1 then 'Implement process improvement (e.g., Six Sigma DMAIC) to reduce defect rate.'
if costPerSample > 100 then 'Negotiate with lab or use alternative analysis methods (e.g., NDT).'
if numSamples > 0.5 * batchSize then 'Consider 100% inspection or reduce sample size using statistical sampling.'
if costSavings < 0 then 'Increase sample size or improve defect detection to reduce total cost.'
